business.headline: IRDAI revamps key advisory committee, brings in industry and policy expertsThe reconstituted panel includes several key institutional representatives. These include members from the Life Insurance Council and the General Insurance Council, along with heads of important professional bodies.
Sensex ends 568 points higher, Nifty above 23,500; Eternal up 6%The S&P BSE Sensex jumped 567.99 points to close at 76,070.84, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 172.35 points to end at 23,581.15.

The reasons aren't always clear, and insurers don't always explain them. In this article, we break down the possible reasons behind these sudden premium jumps.

From ITR revision to office commute relief: 5 big tax changes from April 1Starting April 1, several tax rules are set to change in ways that could directly impact your filing and savings. From ITR revisions to commute benefits, here's a quick look at what's new.

GSP Crop Science IPO: should you subscribe to the public listing? Check GMPGSP Crop Science IPO is a book-built issue of Rs 400.00 crore. It includes a fresh issue of 75 lakh shares worth Rs 240.00 crore and an offer for sale of 50 lakh shares worth Rs 160.00 crore.

Should married couples be allowed to file taxes together in India?In most homes, finances are shared. But when it comes to taxes, couples are treated as individuals. Should that change? A new idea is now putting this question in focus.

How goal-based investing can transform savers into long-term wealth-builders?Goal-based investing reframes personal finance from a pursuit of returns into a pursuit of readiness. The most meaningful financial success is not the highest number on a statement, but the confidence that money will be available when life demands it.

Power stocks rise today; Adani Power gains 2% after 1,600 MW deal winThe buying interest was visible across the sector, led by stocks such as Tata Power, NLC India, Torrent Power and Adani Power. Among the top movers, Adani Power remained in focus after a fresh business development.

West Asia conflict triggers global helium shortage. Why it's no laughing matterCrude oil and LNG have been in the limelight amid the ongoing West Asia war. But another critical gas, helium, is now facing a global supply shock and here's why it's bad news.
Sensex, Nifty open flat as crude oil prices weigh in on investor sentimentThe S&P BSE Sensex gained 63.36 points to 75,566.21, while the NSE Nifty50 added 21.90 points to 23,430.70 as of 9:42 am.

Explained: Why gold prices are not rising despite ongoing Iran warIt is unusual for gold to fall during volatile situations like war. But that is exactly what is happening now and it raises a key question about what is driving prices today

Tenant vs landlord: How India's new rental rules change the balanceRenting a home in India has long been a game of negotiation, where rules were often unclear and power rarely equal between tenants and landlords. A new rental framework based on the Model Tenancy Act, 2021 is trying to formalise how homes are rented in India.

Live: Amid LPG concerns, govt pushes shift to PNGLPG crisis live updates: Amid shortage fears triggered by ongoing conflict in West Asia, fresh supplies are beginning to arrive in India. After the Shivalik vessel reached on Monday, another LPG carrier, Nanda Devi, has now arrived at Gujarat's Vadinar port with over 47,000 metric tonnes, easing immediate pressure. Even so, the impact of earlier disruptions continues to be felt across sectors, with cities like Pune reporting strain and limited cafeteria operations. Authorities have also intensified action against hoarding, with raids, FIRs and arrests reported across states, while the government pushes commercial users to shift to piped natural gas and maintains there is no nationwide shortage.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market fall or rise today?Early signals suggest a positive start. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,515.50 at around 8:00 am. This points to the Nifty 50 opening above Monday's closing level of 23,408.8.

How young professionals are piling up Rs 10–50 lakh debt through small EMIsIt begins with a small EMI—a new phone, a quick getaway, but for many young professionals, these tiny payments are quietly adding up to tens of lakhs. Are they prepared to handle it?

Gold drops over Rs 3,400, silver cheaper by Rs 11,000: Is it time to invest?Despite global tensions, gold and silver turned cheaper, with gold down Rs 3,400 and silver down Rs 11,000. Does this decline signal a good entry point for investors?

Government urges LPG users to switch to PNG as companies roll out incentivesSujata Sharma, Joint Secretary in the Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas, said the move has been discussed over the past few days as authorities look to ensure stable fuel availability across the country.
Sensex ends 939 points higher, Nifty above 23,300; SBI up 2%The S&P BSE Sensex was up 938.93 points to end at 75,502.85, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 257.70 points to close at 23,408.80.

Saudi Arabia offers alternate Red Sea route as Strait of Hormuz faces crisisSaudi Arabia is now giving its long-term customers the option to receive their April oil allocations through the Red Sea port of Yanbu instead of the usual Persian Gulf terminals.

entertainment.headline: Kangana Ranaut on Nora Fatehi song: Bollywood has crossed all limits of vulgarity Kangana Ranaut has criticised Bollywood for its increasing vulgarity following the controversy over Nora Fatehi's song from KD – The Devil. She emphasised the need for stricter regulations to curb obscene content in films and music videos.

Silambarasaran shares BTS still from Arasan shoot, second schedule underway Actor Silambarasan is back on the sets of Arasan, with the second shooting schedule progressing fast in Chennai.  Fans are eagerly waiting for the film, which is part of Vetri Maaran's Vada Chennai universe.

Nora Fatehi song Sarke Chunar row: Cine worker body seeks action from I&B, censors All Indian Cine Workers Association has called on the CBFC and the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting to take strict action against the song Sarke Chunar Teri Sarke. In their letter, they cited public outrage and negative impact on youth.

Ali Fazal marks anniversary with sweet message for Richa Chadha: The rest is served Ali Fazal shared a sweet anniversary note for Richa Chadha along with candid pictures from their journey together. He called the day special and memorable.

Diljit Dosanjh's Main Vaapas Aaunga teaser in theatres with Dhurandhar 2? Imtiaz Ali hinted at the teaser for Main Vaapas Aaunga, featuring Diljit Dosanjh, being attached with Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge.

Divyanka Tripathi, Vivek Dahiya to welcome first child after 10 years: Source Television actors Divyanka Tripathi and Vivek Dahiya are preparing to welcome their first child after 10 years of marriage, a source close to the actors confirmed to India Today.

The Great Grand Superhero teaser: Grandpa gets superpowers in Jackie Shroff film Jackie Shroff unveiled the first look of The Great Grand Superhero – Aliens Ka Aagman. The film promises a story based on childhood imagination and fantasy.

Gaurav Kapur gets emotional as Kritika Kamra walks in at wedding. Watch video Gaurav Kapur and Kritika Kamra recently got married in a private ceremony. A new video emerging from the March 11 wedding captures heartfelt moments and vows shared between the couple.

I am not a Pakistani: Dhurandhar actor defends hijacker role amid backlash Vivek Sinha, who plays a hijacker in Dhurandhar, has reacted to the backlash over his role in Aditya Dhar's film. In a message shared on social media, the actor clarified that he is neither a terrorist nor a Pakistani.

Panchayat's Faisal Malik cast as Kumbhakaran in Nitesh Tiwari's Ramayana: Report Actor Faisal Malik has reportedly been roped in to play Kumbhakaran in Nitesh Tiwari's Ramayana. The film is set to release this Diwali and promises a grand retelling of the epic.

Ballet and opera have defeated Timothee Chalamet: Jon Stewart Jon Stewart reacted to Timothee Chalamet's comments about people no longer caring about ballet and opera. He said that the classical arts defeated Chalamet in a "knockout."

BB19's Tanya Mittal thanks Ektaa Kapoor in emotional note: Mumbai feels like home Tanya Mittal shared a video from Ektaa Kapoor's Hoonur launch party, showing the two dancing and enjoying themselves. She also posted a note thanking Ektaa for her support and for making Mumbai feel like home.

Nora Fatehi's KD song row: National Human Rights Commission issues notice to team The National Human Rights Commission has issued a notice to the makers of KD - The Devil over alleged use of vulgar and suggestive song lyrics. The controversial track has been removed from social media following public backlash.

Zendaya reacts to viral AI wedding pics with Tom Holland: Many were fooled Zendaya reacted to viral AI-generated wedding photos with Tom Holland, revealing that many people believed they were real and sparking fresh curiosity about the rumours.

Ayushmann Khurrana shares unheard version of Saadi Galli from Nautanki Saala. Video Ayushmann Khurrana shared a never-heard-before verse of Saadi Galli from Nautanki Saala! after being inspired by fan videos on social media.

Just don't tolerate disrespect: Teyana Taylor on security guard row at Oscars Actor Teyana Taylor reacted to the altercation with security at the Academy Awards. In a statement, the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences also expressed regret over the incident that the actor had to endure.

Nora Fatehi's KD song taken down after allegations of vulgar lyrics The makers of the Kannada film, KD: The Devil, have made the Nora Fatehi song private after widespread allegations of suggestive lyrics and visuals.  The National Human Rights Commission has reportedly issued notices to the filmmakers following backlash.

Busy with film names: Onir's dig at CBFC silence over new Nora Fatehi song Filmmaker Onir questioned the CBFC's priorities amid backlash over Nora Fatehi and Sanjay Dutt's 'Sarke Chunar' lyrics. The row has sparked a wider debate on censorship and content regulation.

Indie film Dihadi makers flag similarities with Homebound: Resemblances are uncanny The makers of the indie film Dihadi have pointed out striking visual similarities with the Neeraj Ghaywan's Homebound, sparking conversations on social media.

general news.headline: Dilli ka laddu will not win: Mamata to lock horns with Suvendu in BhabanipurThe Trinamool Congress announced its candidates for the West Bengal Assembly elections, declaring it will contest 291 of the 294 seats while leaving three constituencies for its ally BGPM.

Larijani becomes the most senior Iranian official to be killed after Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

Celina Jaitly's brother says he doesn't want to speak to her, court closes caseThe Delhi High Court closed actor Celina Jaitly's plea after her brother Vikrant Jaitly, detained in the UAE, refused communication and legal aid. The court noted he has had multiple consular meetings and will let his wife decide future steps.

How BJP is trying to trap Mamata in Bhabanipur ChakravyuhThe BJP has strategised to bog down Mamata Banerjee in her Bhabanipur seat by fielding West Bengal LOP, Suvendu Adhikari, from the Kolkata seat. This is why the Bhabanipur battle is a Chakravyuh for Mamata and her Trinamool Congress.

Why Iran is hitting UAE the hardest?The UAE has faced the most intense barrage of Iranian attacks among the Gulf countries since the US-Israel war on Tehran began. Civilian and business infrastructure in Dubai and other hubs have been targeted. But why has the UAE been targeted more than its Gulf neighbours? Here's what's driving Iran's aggression against it.

Is protein water worth the hype?Hydrolysed protein, often known as protein water, is a novel type of protein supplement that is becoming popular. We asked experts about its advantages, benefits and claims it makes about gaining muscle.

Live: War will end in weeks, not months; Trump won't back down, says White House officialUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The Middle East conflict continues to escalate with fresh developments across the region. US President Donald Trump said Washington was "shocked" after Iran began striking Gulf countries including Kuwait and Bahrain, widening the war's scope. Meanwhile, tensions have intensified around the Strait of Hormuz as several US allies declined Trump's call to deploy warships to escort oil tankers. Israel also reported new Iranian missile launches, while global markets remain on edge amid fears of prolonged disruption to energy supplies. Follow indiatoday.in for the latest live updates.

Contrary to truth: Rajinikanth rejects TVK leader's claim of DMK threatRajinikanth dismissed as 'slanderous' and 'contrary to the truth' allegations by a TVK leader that the DMK had threatened him during his planned political entry.

You Indian? Get lost: Are Indians treated badly in Vietnam?Vietnam has grown popular among Indian travellers, but some say they faced hostility in shops and markets. Tourists and locals suggest stereotypes about Indians may be contributing to the friction.

This is what Artemis-II astronauts will do on their trip to the MoonThe 10-day journey, which will send the four astronauts around the Moon and back, is packed with critical tests, scientific observations, and preparations for future lunar landings.

Nato's 'nay' to Trump: Why Europe wants no part of America's war on IranThe conflict initiated by Donald Trump has escalated into a difficult war with Iran, but European Nato allies have largely refused to support it, arguing it is not their responsibility and prioritising their own security concerns. Europe's reluctance stems from lack of consultation, domestic opposition, and fear of weakening defenses against Russia, while instead pushing for a diplomatic resolution.

Started gym at 38, won Mr India, this Bihar man now runs a naan stall in DelhiA street vendor, who began bodybuilding at 38, has won the Mr India Classic title while continuing to run a naan stall to support himself in Delhi.

Rashmika Mandanna turned stacked gold kadhas into the ultimate haldi statementThere hasn't been a single dull fashion moment from Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda's wedding, and their haldi photos prove exactly that.

Live: Pak strike on Kabul rehab kills 408; China expresses concern, calls for ceasefirePakistan-Afghanistan Conflict Live Updates: At least 400 people were killed and 250 others injured in an airstrike allegedly carried out by Pakistan on a drug rehabilitation hospital in Kabul, the Afghan Taliban government said on Tuesday.

Pakistan, however, rejected the allegation as "false and misleading", maintaining that its strikes on Monday night were precise and targeted only military installations and terrorist support infrastructure. Stay tuned to India Today for the latest updates on the Pakistan-Afghanistan conflict.

After the Shivalik vessel reached on Monday, another LPG carrier, Nanda Devi, has now arrived at Gujarat's Vadinar port with over 47,000 metric tonnes, easing immediate pressure. Even so, the impact of earlier disruptions continues to be felt across sectors, with cities like Pune reporting strain and limited cafeteria operations. Authorities have also intensified action against hoarding, with raids, FIRs and arrests reported across states, while the government pushes commercial users to shift to piped natural gas and maintains there is no nationwide shortage.

Not Mamata Banerjee, Suvendu Adhikari may take on his ex-loyalist in NandigramPabitra Kar, who previously served as the panchayat pradhan of Boyal-1 gram panchayat in Nandigram-2 block of Purba Medinipur district, joined the Trinamool in the presence of party leader Abhishek Banerjee.

Menstrual leave debate returns: Why women must hide biology to succeedThe debate around menstrual leave has resurfaced after the Supreme Court said mandating it may discourage companies from hiring women. But the controversy raises a deeper question: why are women still expected to ignore biological realities and work within systems designed almost entirely around male physiology?

What have you done? Cousin screams as Delhi man shoots himself while filming reelThe man loaded the magazine, cocked the pistol, briefly looked at the camera, and then shot himself in the left side of his chest.

Is Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu dead or alive?Over the past week, social media has been abuzz with rumours claiming that Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu was killed in an Iranian missile strike. However, there remains no credible evidence. It coincided with his reported absence from cabinet meetings. However, Israel has released videos and images to assert that Netanyahu is alive.

Dressing up massacre as military op: India slams Pak for Kabul hospital attackThe strike on a drug rehabilitation hospital in Kabul, which killed over 400 and left nearly 300 injured, comes amid a sharp escalation in the conflict between Pakistan and Afghanistan.

sports.headline: Conway sparks carnage as New Zealand crush South Africa to win 2nd T20I by 68 runs Devon Conway's 60 and a fiery pace display from Ben Sears and Lockie Ferguson helped New Zealand crush South Africa by 68 runs, levelling the T20I series after a dramatic collapse.

Sensational Amelia Kerr scripts history with 100 Women's T20I wickets NZW vs SAW: New Zealand skipper Amelia Kerr became the fastest cricketer from a full-member nation to complete 1,000 runs and 100 wickets in Women's T20Is, reaching the milestone in her 94th match against South Africa.

Experience then, fiery passion now: Suryakumar Yadav on India's evolution in T20Is T20 World Cup: Suryakumar Yadav compares India's 2024 and 2026 T20 World Cup wins, highlighting the shift from experienced composure to youthful intensity and how home pressure shaped the title defence.

KKR handed major scare as Harshit Rana set to miss most of IPL 2026 IPL 2026: Kolkata Knight Riders have been handed a big injury scare. Fast bowler Harshit Rana, who was set to lead the team's attack, is most likely to miss most of the tournament. According to reports, Rana is not going to recover in time from his knee injury.

IPL 2026 injury worries grow: Cummins, Hazlewood doubtful for opening games Sunrisers Hyderabad captain Pat Cummins and RCB pacer Josh Hazlewood are doubtful for the opening games of IPL 2026. Both Australian quicks are still awaiting medical clearance.

FIFA World Cup to suffer same fate as cricket? Iran urges games to be moved out of USA Iran's football federation is pushing to move its 2026 World Cup matches from the US to Mexico over safety fears, in a controversy drawing comparisons to Bangladesh's T20 World Cup crisis.

Tilak Varma felicitated by Telangana CM Revanth Reddy after T20 World Cup triumph India T20 World Cup winner Tilak Varma received a warm welcome in his home state after the tournament triumph. Telangana Chief Minister A Revanth Reddy felicitated the young batter for his role in India's historic title defence.

Is CSK forcing a spot for MS Dhoni? De Villiers puzzled by veteran's role in IPL 2026 IPL 2026: AB de Villiers questions MS Dhoni's role at CSK in IPL 2026, saying the legend must bat higher or risk playing for the wrong reasons as Sanju Samson's arrival reshapes Chennai's lineup.

IPL as big as international cricket: Ricky Ponting explains India's huge talent pool Ricky Ponting believes the Indian Premier League plays a major role in shaping India's T20 success. The former Australia captain said IPL matches are as intense as international games and help prepare players for the national team.

Neymar focused on FIFA World Cup dream despite another Brazil squad snub Neymar's hopes of playing at the 2026 FIFA World Cup remain alive despite another Brazil squad snub. The Santos forward admitted he was disappointed after being left out of Brazil's upcoming friendlies in the United States.

Watch: Rohit Sharma in top gear in MI nets session ahead of IPL 2026IPL 2026: Rohit Sharma has already started turning heads ahead of IPL 2026. A viral video from Mumbai Indians' training session showed the former India captain going after the bowlers, raising excitement among fans before the new season.

A war crime: Afghanistan cricketers slam deadly Pak strikes on Kabul hospitalAfghanistan cricket stars Rashid Khan and Mohammad Nabi have strongly condemned reported Pakistani airstrikes in Kabul. The two players expressed grief and anger over civilian casualties after a hospital was reportedly struck during the attack.

KKR's fast bowling problem: 2024 winners head towards shaky start to IPL campaignKolkata Knight Riders enter the new IPL season with a fast-bowling unit already battered by injuries and uncertainty. With Harshit Rana unlikely to feature and Matheesha Pathirana still recovering, the defending champions suddenly look short of pace options and experience.

Pratika Rawal opens up on her deep bond with Test cricket: This is my formatPratika Rawal said that she considers Test cricket the ultimate measure of a cricketer's character, patience, and determination. She made her debut in the Day-Night Perth Test against Australia, scoring her maiden fifty.

Chennai Super Kings appoint ex-England wicketkeeper as fielding coach for IPL 2026Chennai Super Kings have appointed former England wicketkeeper-batter James Foster as their new fielding coach ahead of IPL 2026, adding an experienced coach to Stephen Fleming's support staff.

Liam Livingstone slams Brendon McCullum, Rob Key in sensational rant against ECBLiam Livingstone launched a blistering attack on England's team management, accusing Brendon McCullum and Rob Key of poor communication and a lack of support after being dropped from the white-ball setup.

The story of how Suryakumar, not Hardik Pandya, became India's T20I captainSuryakumar Yadav was made the T20I captain after the 2024 World Cup, pipping the then vice-captain Hardik Pandya. The batter reveals how he got to know about the news.

Vaishnavi Adkar continues dream run, closes in on India's No.1 WTA rankingVaishnavi Adkar's rapid rise continued as she broke into the WTA top 400 for the first time, climbing to a career-best No. 397 after winning the ITF W35 Kalaburagi title and moving within six spots of India's No.1 ranking.

Pakistan fast bowler grabs limelight with massive no-ball in National T20 CupPakistan pacer Asad Akhtar grabbed attention after bowling a huge no-ball during Karachi Blues' National T20 Cup clash against Lahore Blues on Saturday at the Imran Khan Cricket Stadium in Peshawar.

Chelsea fined 131 crore rupees, 1-year-ban for breaching financial rulesChelsea have been fined $14.3 million and handed a suspended transfer ban after the Premier League found the club guilty of breaching financial regulations. Undisclosed payments, made between 2011 and 2018 have landed the club in trouble.

It is not Shubman Gill: Fans slam BCCI over huge mistake in Naman AwardsThe Indian Cricket Board is being called out for an extremely silly mistake at the Naman Awards 2026. During the ceremony, BCCI credited Shubman Gill as the captain who won the Champions Trophy, and not Rohit Sharma.

Antonelli arrives, Hamilton makes comeback in scintillating Shanghai showdownShanghai GP: Kimi Antonelli's maiden win, Lewis Hamilton's Ferrari debut podium, and Max Verstappen's frustration headlined a thrilling Chinese Grand Prix as Formula 1's 2026 season bursts into life in Shanghai.

MS Dhoni always has a positive influence on me: Sanju Samson eager for CSK stintSanju Samson said he is excited to share the CSK dressing room with MS Dhoni in IPL 2026, adding that the former India captain has always had a positive influence on him and will be a valuable source of learning.

Unleash the real Hardik Pandya: Harbhajan Singh on what MI needs to win IPL 2026IPL 2026: Harbhajan Singh believes that the Mumbai Indians need to find a way to bring out the best in Hardik Pandya should they stand a chance of getting back to winning titles.

Ricky Ponting explains the bold call that shaped India's T20 WC title defenceRicky Ponting said India's bold call to restore Sanju Samson as opener and move Ishan Kishan to No. 3 played a major role in their successful T20 World Cup 2026 title defence.

Fastest IPL hundred or Chris Gayle's 175? Vaibhav Sooryavanshi makes his pickDuring the Naman Awards on Sunday, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi revealed that he aspires to break Chris Gayle's long-standing record of 175, the highest individual score in T20 cricket.

Buying Pak players contributes to death of Indian soldiers: GavaskarSunil Gavaskar has criticised SunRisers Leeds owners for signing Pakistan spinner Abrar Ahmed at The Hundred 2026 auction, arguing that Indian owners must not contribute to Pakistani government revenues.

No India T20 World Cup cricketer nominated for February's Player of the Month awardDespite India's successful T20 World Cup title defence, no Indian men's cricketer was shortlisted for the ICC Men's Player of the Month award for February. But India have representation in the women's category, with pacer Arundhati Reddy nominated.

India can field two playing XIs: Suryakumar Yadav hails country's talent poolIndia captain Suryakumar Yadav believes the country's T20 talent pool has grown so deep that multiple international-quality teams could be formed. He credits domestic cricket and the IPL for building India's remarkable strength in the format.

Gambir admits mistake in handling relationship with Rohit and KohliWas Gautam Gambhir behind the Test retirements of Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma? India head coach Gautam Gambhir has opened up on the possible mistakes he might have made in handling his relationship with the stalwarts.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
